refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from mission context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Atomicity requires separating these concerns. CR-NGHE-005.1 focuses on the derivation activity—the logical transformation from mission inputs to performance targets—which is the core functional obligation. Recording and management are separate concerns that belong in distinct child FRs. The refined statement remains solution-neutral by specifying 'derive' (a functional verb) without prescribing the mechanism (algorithm, tool, process).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a set of worksite mission inputs (e.g., production targets, terrain, payload, duty cycle), the system produces a documented set of performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to those inputs and satisfy acceptance criteria.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements in a persistent, retrievable format.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall establish traceability between worksite mission inputs and derived performance targets.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Establish a parameterized mission-context intake process that captures worksite operational profile (hazard class, duty cycle, environmental conditions, safety constraints) and maps each input to a corresponding performance target via a documented traceability matrix, with version control for baseline snapshots.

DP-B: Implement a hierarchical performance requirement derivation engine that accepts worksite mission inputs, applies domain-specific inference rules (safety-driven, operational-driven, environmental-driven), and outputs a structured performance specification document with explicit linkage to source constraints.

DP-C: Design a dual-layer requirement capture system: Layer 1 (intake) collects raw worksite context (safety plan excerpts, operational policies, duty cycles); Layer 2 (derivation) applies a rule-based or model-based transformation to produce performance targets, with audit trail for each derivation step.

DP-D: Create a performance baseline registry that stores mission-context snapshots, derived performance targets, and calibration/update events as immutable records, enabling historical traceability and change justification without requiring re-derivation of prior baselines.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Design a dual-layer requirement capture system: Layer 1 (intake) collects raw worksite context (safety plan excerpts, operational policies, duty cycles); Layer 2 (derivation) applies a rule-based or model-based transformation to produce performance targets, with audit trail for each derivation step.

potential_sources: Layer 1 (intake) and Layer 2 (derivation) are logically separable; intake can evolve independently of derivation rules.
Audit trail is orthogonal to both layers and can be implemented as a cross-cutting concern without tight coupling.
Rule-based transformation allows substitution of inference logic (e.g., from heuristic to model-based) without restructuring intake or audit mechanisms.
Minor risk: If derivation rules become tightly bound to specific worksite types, generalizability may suffer; mitigation is to parameterize rules by worksite class.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids premature technology binding (e.g., 'use a database' or 'use a specific inference engine') while maintaining clear separation of concerns. The dual-layer structure supports independent testing, evolution, and ownership boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is sufficiently well-defined in safety plans and operational policies to enable deterministic or semi-deterministic derivation of performance targets. Risk: If context is ambiguous or incomplete, derivation may require human judgment; mitigation is to define escalation rules and expert review gates.

Assumption: A set of derivation rules (or a model) exists or can be developed to map mission context to performance targets. Risk: If no such rules are available, the system becomes a documentation tool rather than an automated derivation engine; mitigation is to prototype rule discovery during requirements refinement.

Assumption: Traceability and audit trail requirements are non-negotiable. Risk: If audit overhead becomes excessive, performance of the intake/derivation process may degrade; mitigation is to design audit logging as a non-blocking, asynchronous service.

Assumption: Baseline snapshots and calibration updates are infrequent enough to be managed manually or via lightweight versioning. Risk: If updates are frequent or concurrent, version control complexity may increase; mitigation is to adopt a formal change control board or automated versioning strategy.

Assumption: The acceptance criteria ('documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintains a history of baselines/calibration updates') can be satisfied by a single integrated system. Risk: If traceability and history management are governed by separate tools or processes, integration complexity increases; mitigation is to define clear data exchange contracts.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005.1 is well-formed and maps cleanly to DP-C. Recommend adding a sub-requirement to clarify the scope of 'derivation rules': (1) Are rules domain-specific (e.g., mining vs. construction vs. manufacturing)? (2) Who owns rule authorship and validation? (3) What is the fallback if a rule cannot be applied? Propose: FR-NGHE-005.1.1: 'The system shall support rule-based derivation of performance targets from mission context, with documented rule provenance and a defined escalation path for ambiguous or out-of-scope inputs.' This will sharpen the DP-to-IM mapping when selecting a specific rule engine or knowledge representation.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Centralized requirement capture, transformation, and audit management for safety-critical performance targets derived from worksite context and operational policies.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw worksite context data (safety plan excerpts, operational policies, duty cycles) from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems for Layer 1 intake.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets and safety constraints to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emits audit trail records and derivation step logs for compliance and traceability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with NGHE Solution for rule-base updates, model refinements, and centralized audit storage.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: Safety Services is the natural home for requirement capture, rule-based/model-based transformation, and audit trail management. Layer 1 (intake) and Layer 2 (derivation) are both safety-critical functions. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to external data sources (Telemetry) and consumers (Tasking, SafetyAlert). Cohesion is high—all components serve safety requirement derivation.

Moderate fit: NGHE Solution could host the rule-base and model logic, but the intake layer (Layer 1) and real-time audit trail generation are operationally coupled to Vehicle/Teleoperation state. Placing the entire DP here would require heavy bidirectional coupling to Safety Services for context collection and to Vehicle/Teleoperation for live derivation. Better suited as a backend rule/model repository accessed by Safety Services.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a platform for execution, not requirement derivation. Layer 1 intake and Layer 2 transformation are orthogonal to vehicle control. Placing DP here would scatter safety requirement logic across the vehicle stack and create tight coupling to teleoperation and external policy sources. High diffusion risk.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is an operational interface for human control, not a requirement derivation engine. Layer 1 and Layer 2 logic would be misplaced here. Teleoperation should consume derived targets from Safety Services, not generate them. Significant diffusion and cohesion violation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the single authoritative module for requirement capture and derivation because: (1) it owns safety-critical logic; (2) it is positioned to aggregate worksite context from multiple sources (Telemetry); (3) it can apply rule-based/model-based transformations in isolation from operational execution; (4) it can maintain a complete audit trail without interference from Vehicle or Teleoperation state machines; (5) it interfaces cleanly to NGHE Solution for rule/model updates and to Tasking/SafetyAlert for downstream consumption.
containment_risks: Risk: Layer 1 intake may require real-time synchronization with Vehicle and Teleoperation telemetry streams. If Telemetry interface is asynchronous or lossy, audit trail completeness could be compromised. Mitigation: define Telemetry contract with guaranteed delivery and timestamping.

Risk: Layer 2 derivation rules may evolve frequently (policy updates, model refinements). If rule updates are not versioned and linked to audit records, traceability could degrade. Mitigation: implement versioned rule repository in NGHE Solution with immutable audit trail in Safety Services.

Risk: Performance targets derived by Safety Services must be enforced by Vehicle and Teleoperation. If enforcement is inconsistent or delayed, the audit trail becomes misleading. Mitigation: establish feedback loop from Vehicle/Teleoperation to Safety Services confirming target adoption.

Risk: Audit trail volume could grow rapidly in high-duty-cycle operations. Storage and query performance in Safety Services may become a bottleneck. Mitigation: design tiered audit storage (hot/cold) with archival to NGHE Solution backend.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criteria require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained history of baselines/calibration updates. Safety Services module boundary explicitly owns Layer 1 (intake) and Layer 2 (derivation) with audit trail generation. Traceability is verifiable via audit trail records emitted on SafetyAlert interface and stored in NGHE Solution backend. Baseline/calibration history is maintainable within Safety Services with versioned rule repository in NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR-CR-NGHE-005.1 is allocated entirely to Safety Services IM with supporting rule/model repository in NGHE Solution. Telemetry provides input data; Tasking and SafetyAlert provide output consumption and audit visibility. No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C correctly decomposes FR-CR-NGHE-005.1 into two functional layers: Layer 1 (intake of raw worksite context) and Layer 2 (rule-based/model-based transformation to performance targets). Dual-layer design with audit trail satisfies the requirement to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context and maintain traceability. DP is logically sound and addresses the functional requirement without over-specification of implementation.

diffusion_risks: Layer 1 intake depends on real-time Telemetry stream synchronization. If Telemetry interface is asynchronous or lossy, audit trail completeness and derivation accuracy could be compromised. Mitigation required: define Telemetry contract with guaranteed delivery semantics, timestamping, and loss detection.

Layer 2 derivation rules are mutable (policy updates, model refinements). Rule versioning and immutable linkage to audit records is critical for traceability. If rule updates are not versioned or audit records do not capture rule version at derivation time, historical audit trail becomes unreliable. Mitigation: implement versioned rule repository in NGHE Solution with rule version ID embedded in every audit record.

Performance targets derived by Safety Services must be enforced by Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. If enforcement is delayed, inconsistent, or rejected, the audit trail records derivation but not actual adoption. This creates a gap between intended and realized safety posture. Mitigation: establish feedback loop from Vehicle/Teleoperation to Safety Services confirming target adoption and enforcement status.

Audit trail volume scales with duty cycle and number of worksite context updates. Storage, query, and retrieval performance in Safety Services may become a bottleneck in high-frequency operations. Mitigation: design tiered audit storage (hot/cold) with archival to NGHE Solution backend and implement query indexing on timestamp and rule version.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Excellent containment fit (0.92 score). Safety Services is the natural authoritative module for DP-C because: (1) it owns safety-critical requirement derivation logic; (2) it is positioned to aggregate worksite context from Telemetry without operational coupling; (3) Layer 1 and Layer 2 are cohesive safety functions; (4) audit trail generation is intrinsic to Safety Services responsibility; (5) interfaces to NGHE Solution (rule/model updates), Telemetry (context intake), and Tasking/SafetyAlert (target/audit output) are clean and unidirectional. Minimal diffusion (0.08 score) reflects only necessary external dependencies. NGHE Solution plays supporting role as rule/model repository, not primary DP owner. Vehicle and Teleoperation are correctly positioned as consumers, not contributors to derivation logic.

recommended_revisions: Formalize Telemetry interface contract: specify guaranteed delivery semantics, maximum latency, timestamping precision, and loss detection mechanism. Define fallback behavior if Telemetry stream is interrupted.

Implement versioned rule repository in NGHE Solution with immutable rule snapshots. Embed rule version ID and timestamp in every Layer 2 derivation audit record to enable historical traceability and root-cause analysis of performance target changes.

Define feedback loop from Vehicle and Teleoperation to Safety Services: establish confirmation protocol for target adoption, enforcement status, and any deviations. Log feedback in audit trail to close the gap between intended and realized safety posture.

Design tiered audit storage architecture: hot storage in Safety Services for recent records (e.g., last 7 days), cold storage in NGHE Solution backend for archival. Implement indexed query capability on timestamp, rule version, and worksite context source.

Establish acceptance test for traceability: verify that every performance target in Tasking output can be traced back through audit trail to specific worksite context inputs and rule version. Verify that baseline/calibration history is queryable and immutable.
Document the contract between Safety Services and NGHE Solution for rule/model updates: specify versioning scheme, update frequency, rollback procedures, and audit trail linkage to rule changes.

decision.justification: FR-CR-NGHE-005.1 passes all three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) with high verification feasibility and clear allocation. However, the gate analysis identifies four critical diffusion risks that require explicit functional decomposition at the next level to ensure each risk is owned by a specific sub-FR and traced to a corresponding DP and IM. The risks are not implementation details—they are functional concerns: (1) Telemetry intake reliability and synchronization, (2) Rule versioning and audit immutability, (3) Target adoption feedback and enforcement closure, and (4) Audit storage scalability and queryability. Each risk maps to a distinct functional responsibility that should be decomposed as a sub-FR to maintain traceability and enable independent verification. High safety criticality reinforces the need for explicit functional decomposition rather than deferring these concerns to detailed design.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-CR-NGHE-005.1.1: Ensure Telemetry intake stream is synchronized, timestamped, and loss-detected to guarantee completeness and accuracy of worksite context inputs to Layer 1 derivation.
FR-CR-NGHE-005.1.2: Maintain immutable, versioned rule repository with rule version ID embedded in every Layer 2 derivation audit record to enable historical traceability and root-cause analysis.
FR-CR-NGHE-005.1.3: Establish feedback loop from Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems to Safety Services confirming adoption, enforcement status, and deviations of derived performance targets.

FR-CR-NGHE-005.1.4: Provide queryable, tiered audit storage (hot/cold) with indexed retrieval on timestamp, rule version, and worksite context source to support audit trail scalability and forensic analysis.

revise.why: DP-C (Layer 1 intake + Layer 2 rule-based derivation + audit trail) is logically sound but does not explicitly decompose the four diffusion risks into distinct functional layers. Revise DP-C to include: (1) Telemetry contract enforcement (guaranteed delivery, timestamping, loss detection), (2) Rule versioning and immutable audit linkage, (3) Feedback loop for target adoption confirmation, and (4) Tiered audit storage with indexed query. These should be reflected as sub-DPs corresponding to the sub-FRs above. This ensures that each risk is not buried in implementation but is explicitly addressed as a functional design choice traceable to a requirement.

assumptions: Telemetry interface can be contractually bound to provide guaranteed delivery semantics and timestamping precision required by Layer 1.
NGHE Solution backend has sufficient capacity and query performance to support versioned rule repository and cold audit storage.
Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems can implement feedback protocol to confirm target adoption without introducing unacceptable latency or coupling.
Audit trail volume and query patterns are predictable enough to design effective tiered storage and indexing strategy.
Safety Services module has authority to enforce rule versioning and audit immutability across all derivation operations.
open_risks: Telemetry stream asynchronicity or loss could compromise audit trail completeness if intake contract is not formally defined and enforced. Mitigation: formalize Telemetry interface contract with guaranteed delivery semantics and loss detection before implementation.

Rule updates without versioning or audit linkage could render historical audit trail unreliable and break traceability. Mitigation: implement versioned rule repository with immutable snapshots and rule version ID embedded in every audit record.

Gap between intended and realized safety posture if performance targets are derived but not adopted or enforced by Vehicle/Teleoperation. Mitigation: establish feedback loop with confirmation protocol and log adoption status in audit trail.

Audit storage and query performance may degrade under high-frequency operations if tiered architecture and indexing are not designed upfront. Mitigation: design tiered storage (hot/cold) with archival and implement indexed query on timestamp, rule version, and context source.

Acceptance test for traceability may fail if audit trail does not capture sufficient context (rule version, worksite inputs, derivation timestamp) to enable root-cause analysis. Mitigation: define acceptance test upfront and verify audit record schema includes all required fields.
evidence_needed: Formal Telemetry interface contract specifying guaranteed delivery semantics, maximum latency, timestamping precision (e.g., ±10ms), and loss detection mechanism (e.g., sequence numbers, heartbeat).
Versioned rule repository design document in NGHE Solution with immutable rule snapshots, version ID scheme, and audit record schema showing rule version ID and timestamp linkage.
Feedback loop protocol specification between Safety Services and Vehicle/Teleoperation subsystems defining confirmation message format, adoption status codes, and enforcement deviation reporting.
Tiered audit storage architecture design with hot/cold storage thresholds, archival schedule, and indexed query capability on timestamp, rule version, and worksite context source.

Acceptance test specification for traceability: test cases verifying that every performance target in Tasking output can be traced back through audit trail to specific worksite context inputs, rule version, and derivation timestamp.
Baseline/calibration history queryability test: verify that historical rule versions and their associated audit records are retrievable and immutable.
Load test results for audit storage and query performance under expected duty cycle and worksite context update frequency.
